title: Emrusolmin for use in treating multiple system atrophy
abstract: The disclosure provides methods of stabilizing one or more symptoms of Multiple System Atrophy (MSA), limiting the clinical progression of MSA, slowing the clinical progression of MSA, o stabilizing the clinical progression of MSA in a human or population of humans in need thereof, comprising orally administering, on a daily basis, of 300 mg emrusolmin to the human or humans. The disclosure also provide methods of improving overall survival in a human with MSA, improving progress-free survival in a human with MSA, or treating a human having Multiple System Atrophy (MSA) and in need of treatment thereof, comprising orally administering about 300 mg of emrusolmin to the human.
claims:
Claims What is claimed: 1. Emrusolmin for use in: (a) stabilizing one or more symptoms of Multiple System Atrophy (MSA) in a human in need of stabilization thereof, characterized in that 300 mg emrusolmin is orally administered, on a daily basis, and the stabilization is determined as a change from baseline in the humanâs Unified Multiple System Atrophy Rating Scale (UMSARS) score, or a modification thereof; or (b) limiting the clinical progression of MSA in a human in need of limitation thereof, characterized in that 300 mg emrusolmin is orally administered, on a daily basis and the limiting is determined as a change from baseline in the humanâs Unified Multiple System Atrophy Rating Scale (UMSARS) score, or a modification thereof; or (c) slowing the clinical progression of MSA in a human in need of slowing thereof, characterized in that 300 mg emrusolmin is orally administered, on a daily basis, and the slowing is determined as a change from baseline in the humanâs Unified Multiple System Atrophy Rating Scale (UMSARS) score, or a modification thereof; or (d) stabilizing the clinical progression, limiting the clinical progression, and/or slowing the clinical progression of MSA in a human in need thereof, characterized in that 300 mg emrusolmin is orally administered, on a daily basis, and the clinical progression is determined as a change from baseline in the humanâs total UMSARS score of part I and part II combined, or a modification thereof; or (e) stabilizing the clinical progression, limiting the clinical progression, and/or slowing the clinical progression of MSA in a human in need thereof, characterized in that 300 mg emrusolmin is orally administered, on a daily basis, and the clinical progression is determined as a change from baseline in the humanâs CGLS score; or (f) of stabilizing the clinical progression, limiting the clinical progression, and/or slowing the clinical progression of MSA in a human in need thereof, characterized in that 300 mg emrusolmin is orally administered, on a daily basis and the clinical progression is determined as a change from baseline in the humanâs MSA-QoL score; or (g) treating a human having Multiple System Atrophy (MSA) and in need of treatment thereof, characterized in that the treatment comprises a treatment period, comprising 300 mg of 

emrusolmin is orally administered to the human, once daily, for a period of treatment, the administration results in a reduced rate of progression of the humanâs MSA symptoms during the treatment period, the reduced rate of progression is as compared to a placebo treatment, and the rate of progression of MSA symptoms is measured using a (a) Unified Multiple System Atrophy Rating Scale (UMSARS) score, or a modification thereof; (b) total UMSARS score, or a modification thereof; (c) CGI-S; and/or (d) MSA-QoL; or (h) improving overall survival or progress-free survival in a human with MSA characterized in that 300 mg emrusolmin is orally administered, on a daily basis, characterized in that the improvement in overall survival or progress-free is relative to a human with MSA who is not receiving treatment with emrusolmin; or (i) stabilizing, limiting and/or slowing the clinical progression of MSA in a patient in need of stabilization, limitation and/or slowing thereof, characterized in that the patient is being administered one or more moderate or strong CYP1A2 inhibitors, said one or more moderate or strong CYP1 A2 inhibitors is discontinued, and 300 mg of emrusolmin is administered to the patient, on a daily basis; or (j) stabilizing, limiting and/or slowing the clinical progression of MSA in a patient in need of stabilization, limitation and/or slowing thereof, characterized in that the patient is also in need one or more moderate or strong CYP1A2 inhibitors, 300 mg of emrusolmin is administered, on a daily basis, and concomitant administration of said one or more moderate or strong inhibitors CYP1A2 is avoided; or (k) treating a patient in need of emrusolmin therapy, and in need of therapy with one or more moderate or strong inhibitors of CYP1 A2, characterized in that the treating comprises avoiding, contraindicating, or discontinuing concomitant use or co-administration of said one or more moderate or strong CYP1A2 inhibitors; or (l) treating a patient in need of emrusolmin therapy, and in need of therapy with one or more moderate inhibitors of CYP1 A2, characterized in that the treating comprises avoiding, contraindicating, or discontinuing concomitant use or co-administration of said one or more moderate CYP1A2 inhibitors; or (m) treating a patient in need of emrusolmin therapy, and in need of therapy with one or more moderate or strong inhibitors of CYP1A2, while avoiding an adverse drug-drug interaction 

in said patient, characterized in that the treating comprises avoiding, contraindicating, or discontinuing concomitant use or co-administration of said one or more moderate or strong CYP1 A2 inhibitors so that an adverse drug-drug interaction is avoided in said patient. 2. The emrusolmin for use of claim 1 , wherein the emrusolmin administration does not result in a serious adverse event in the human. 3. The emrusolmin for use of claim 1 or 2, wherein the stabilizing, limiting, or slowing is relative to a patient population with MSA that is not receiving treatment with emrusolmin. 4. The emrusolmin for use of any one of claims 1-3, wherein emrusolmin reduces the clinical progression by at least about 20%, such as about 40 to about 60%, or about 30 to about 40% relative to a treatment for MSA lacking emrusolmin. 5. The emrusolmin for use of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the clinical progression comprises worsening MSA symptoms. 6. The emrusolmin for use of any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the human received at least one therapy for the treatment of MSA prior to administration of emrusolmin. 7. The emrusolmin for use of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the human is resistant to treatment with levodopa or has acquired resistance to treatment with levodopa. 8. The emrusolmin for use of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the MSA is multiple system atrophy-parkinsonian type (MSA-P) or multiple system atrophy-cerebellar subtype (MSA-C). 9. The emrusolmin for use of any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the human is diagnosed with MSA-P or MSA-C. 10. The emrusolmin for use of any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the patient is being administered one or more moderate or strong CYP1 A2 inhibitors, wherein the treatment comprises (a) first discontinuing administration of said one or more moderate or strong 

CYP1 A2 inhibitors, and then (b) administering to the patient, on a daily basis, 300 mg of emrusolmin. 11. The emrusolmin for use of any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the human is in need of one or more moderate or strong CYP1 A2 inhibitors, wherein the treatment comprises (a) administering, on a daily basis, 300 mg of emrusolmin and (b) avoiding concomitant administration of said one or more moderate or strong CYP1 A2 inhibitors. 12. The emrusolmin for use of any one of the preceding claims, characterized in that the patient is administered a weak CYP1 A2 inhibitor, wherein the patient is also in need of said weak CYP1A2 inhibitor. 13. The emrusolmin for use according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more moderate or strong CYP1A2 inhibitors is avoided or discontinued for at least 4 days, 2 weeks, 4 weeks before the emrusolmin is administered. 14. The emrusolmin for use according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the CYP1A2 inhibitor is pipemidic acid, mexiletine, fexinidazole, methoxsalen (8- methoxypsoralen), capmatinib, metamizole (dipyrone), vemurafenib, rucaparib, osilodrostat, ritlecitinib, etintidine, phenylpropanolamine, hormonal contraceptives, vemurafenib, genistein, propranolol, troleandomycin, ciprofloxacin, rofecoxib, fluvoxamine, ciprofloxacin, clinafloxacin, idrocilamide, enoxacin, clinafloxacin, viloxazine - high/supratherapeutic dose, oltipraz, or a combination thereof, or the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is pipemidic acid, mexiletine, fexinidazole, methoxsalen (8-methoxypsoralen), capmatinib, metamizole (dipyrone), vemurafenib, rucaparib, osilodrostat, ritlecitinib, etintidine, phenylpropanolamine, hormonal contraceptives, vemurafenib, genistein, propranolol, troleandomycin, or a combination thereof, or the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is fluvoxamine, or the CYP1A2 inhibitor is ciprofloxacin, or the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is mexiletine, or the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is pipemidic acid, or the CYP1A2 inhibitor is fexinidazole, or the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is methoxsalen (8-methoxypsoralen), or the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is capmatinib, or the CYP1A2 inhibitor is metamizole (dipyrone), or the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is vemurafenib, or the CYP1A2 inhibitor is rucaparib, or the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is osilodrostat, the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is ritlecitinib, the CYP1A2 inhibitor is etintidine, the CYP1A2 inhibitor is 

phenylpropanolamine, the CYP1A2 inhibitor is hormonal contraceptives, the CYP1A2 inhibitor is vemurafenib, the CYP1A2 inhibitor is genistein, the CYP1A2 inhibitor is propranolol, the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is troleandomycin, or the CYP1A2 inhibitor is rofecoxib, the CYP1A2 inhibitor is clinafloxacin, the CYP1A2 inhibitor is idrocilamide, the CYP1A2 inhibitor is enoxacin, the CYP1 A2 inhibitor is viloxazine - high/supratherapeutic dose, or the CYP1A2 inhibitor is oltipraz.
